title: penstemon dimorphus
intro: Penstemon dimorphus, also known as the two-colored beardtongue, is a species of flowering plant in the plantain family. It is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Penstemon dimorphus can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in tinctures and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
